name: Dana Morales
technical_background: Strong SQL and BI skills from 6 years of healthcare analytics. Builds dashboards and operational reports. Some Python exposure from online courses but not production-level. Understands HIPAA and clinical workflow basics from hospital system work.

current_profile.summary: Healthcare Data Analyst at a mid-size hospital system with 6 years of experience in clinical and operational analytics. Builds dashboards and operational reports using Power BI and SQL for departments including nursing, finance, and quality. Strong SQL skills including complex joins, CTEs, and window functions for patient cohort analysis. Understands HIPAA compliance requirements and clinical workflow basics from working directly with clinicians. Has used outputs of an existing readmission risk model but has not built or trained ML models. Started learning Python through online courses (Pandas basics) but has not used it professionally. Uses ChatGPT occasionally for drafting reports and exploring data questions.
technical_skills: SQL (advanced: CTEs, window functions, stored procedures)
ETL concepts and data quality checks
soft_skills: Healthcare domain knowledge and clinical workflows
Stakeholder communication with clinicians and administrators
current_profile.ai_experience: Has used outputs of existing ML models (readmission risk scores) and understands their clinical application. Uses ChatGPT for drafting report narratives and exploring data transformation approaches. Attended a hospital webinar on AI in healthcare but has not built, trained, or evaluated ML models. Comfortable interpreting model outputs but not creating them.

learning_intent: Wants to apply ML to improve patient outcomes and hospital operations, specifically in readmission prediction, length-of-stay forecasting, and staffing optimization. Goal: move from descriptive analytics to predictive modeling, learn to build and validate models on healthcare data, and understand responsible deployment in HIPAA-regulated settings. Wants to eventually lead the analytics team's transition to predictive and prescriptive capabilities.

target_jd.title: Healthcare Data Scientist
requirements: Build predictive models on EHR and operations data; validate and monitor performance over time
Develop feature engineering strategies for clinical data and evaluate model bias and drift
Collaborate with clinicians and IT to deploy models into clinical workflow (Epic, Cerner integrations)
Document and communicate insights for clinical and operational stakeholders
Design and implement evaluation frameworks for model accuracy, fairness, and clinical safety
Ensure compliance with HIPAA, IRB, and healthcare-specific data governance requirements
Build data pipelines for model training and inference using Python and cloud tools
Apply NLP techniques to clinical notes, discharge summaries, and unstructured healthcare data
Create dashboards and monitoring systems for deployed model performance
Partner with clinical leadership to identify high-impact AI use cases and measure ROI

expected_skill_gaps.description: Python data stack (Pandas/NumPy for production use) and backend/data pipeline basics for model serving.
Embeddings for clinical NLP, model limitations and reliability, and privacy/bias basics specific to healthcare AI.
Model evaluation concepts, tracing/observability for deployed models, quality dashboards, and release gates for clinical AI.
PII/PHI handling beyond current HIPAA knowledge, data retention policies, and minimization practices for AI training data.
Clinical risk awareness for AI decision support and evidence synthesis for AI-assisted clinical recommendations.
Understanding when to customize vs use off-the-shelf models, and data requirements for fine-tuning on clinical data.
Data leakage prevention and output security for patient-facing or clinician-facing AI tools.
Understanding LLMOps concepts for deploying and monitoring models in clinical settings.
